concepts.title: Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction
concepts.description: Faraday's Law states that the induced emf in a closed loop is equal to the negative rate of change of magnetic flux through the loop. This is a fundamental concept in electromagnetic induction and is often tested in exams.

concepts.title: Lenz's Law
concepts.description: Lenz's Law states that the direction of the induced current is such that it opposes the change in magnetic flux that produced it. This law helps in determining the direction of the induced current.

concepts.title: Induced Emf and Current
concepts.description: The induced emf (ε) is given by ε = -N(dΦ/dt), where N is the number of turns, Φ is the magnetic flux, and t is time. The induced current (I) is given by I = ε/R, where R is the resistance of the circuit.

concepts.title: Right-Hand Rule
concepts.description: To remember the direction of the induced current, use the right-hand rule: thumb points in the direction of the changing magnetic field, fingers curl in the direction of the induced current.

concepts.title: Magnetic Flux Calculation
concepts.description: Students often forget to consider the area vector (A) when calculating the magnetic flux (Φ = BA cosθ). Make sure to include the area vector and the angle between the magnetic field and the area vector.

concepts.title: Magnetic Flux and Induced Emf Units
concepts.description: Remember that the unit of magnetic flux (Φ) is weber (Wb) and the unit of induced emf (ε) is volt (V). The unit of magnetic field (B) is tesla (T) and the unit of area (A) is square meter (m²).

concepts.title: Self-Induction and Mutual Induction
concepts.description: Self-induction occurs when a changing current in a coil induces an emf in the same coil, while mutual induction occurs when a changing current in one coil induces an emf in another coil. Understand the difference between these two types of induction.

concepts.title: Energy Conservation in Electromagnetic Induction
concepts.description: The law of conservation of energy applies to electromagnetic induction. The energy transferred to the circuit is equal to the energy lost by the changing magnetic field.
